title: Impatiens bathiei
intro: Impatiens bathiei, also known as Balsam apple or Bathiei's touch-me-not, is a type of flowering plant in the balsam family (Impatiens) native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating fever, due in part to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Impatiens bathiei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Impatiens bathiei is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for local application.
taxonomy: Impatiens bathiei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Balsamaceae. It is a member of the genus Impatiens, which consists of over 1,000 species of plants. The plant is native to Madagascar. Impatiens bathiei is often referred to as the Madagascar jewelweed due to its vibrant, colorful flowers. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and phylogenetic analysis.
